Off Calendar May-26-2026 Continued To Aug-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carla Allen appeared via zoom for and with Karen Lawrence (administrator).
Jan E. Cherry appeared via zoom (former administrator).

Discussion was held regarding the absence of Ms. Hinshaw, that parties are in need of a hard copy of the Spanish death certificate, and that correspondents were sent on March 10, 2026 to Ms. Allen.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Caroline Hinshaw (attorney) to appear on August 11, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of Final Accounting is also continued to the same date.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Bloom. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Final Accounting By Former Administrator
Off Calendar Feb-17-2026 Continued To May-26-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carla Allen, Esq. for and with Karen Lawrence, Administrator (via zoom).
Caroline Hinshaw, Esq. for and with Jan E Cherry, former Administrator (via zoom).

There has been major miscommunication between the parties. Attorney Carla Allen has been unsuccessful in gathering documentation.

Court notes that Attorney Carla Allen and Administrator Karen Lawrence have done what they were supposed to do regarding letters, bonds issued, etc.

Court previously ordered Attorney Caroline Hinshaw and former Administrator Jan E. Cherry to turn over all estate assets and records. Administrator Karen Lawrence has not received anything to date.

Court grants continuance to get all requested information previously requested documents transferred to Karen Lawrence and her counsel. Communications must be in writing in both directions.

Court orders matter to May-26-2026 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 for status of final accounting, unless they can come to an agreement.

Court orders Attorney Caroline Hinshaw and her client to provide the Death Certificate of Donald Cherry, his Social Security Number, and EINSS4 Form.

Judge:Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: S. Monroe. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Carla Allen for petitioners Karen Lawrence and Christian Cherry, who were also present (via Zoom).
Caroline Hinshaw for suspended administrator Jan Cherry, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Amended Petition for Letters of Administration; and 2) Order to Show Cause regarding removal of the suspended administrator and appointment of a successor administrator.

The Court inquired whether the Ms. Hinshaw's client wished to voluntarily resign as administrator without admitting cause, in order to avoid an involuntary removal reflected in the record. Ms. Hinshaw agreed to this approach and resigned on behalf of her client. The Court accepted this position and confirmed the resignation. The Court took the Order to Show Cause off calendar.

The Court then addressed the petition to appoint Karen Lawrence as successor administrator. The Court indicated its intent to grant the petition, appointing Ms. Lawrence as successor administrator with full IAEA authority, subject to the posting of a $461,000 bond. The Court advised Ms. Allen that, pursuant to Local Rule 14.16(J), a new bond was required even though the amount matched the bond previously posted. Ms. Allen confirmed her understanding. The petition was taken under submission with the intent to grant.

The Court ordered Jan Cherry, through her counsel, to file a final account within 90 days. The Court set a hearing for status on final accounting for February 17, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Off Calendar

Petition For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of Jan Cherry Spears As Administrator
Off Calendar Sep-09-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Carla Allen for petition Karen Lawrence, who was also present (via Zoom).
Caroline Hinshaw for respondent Jan Cherry Spears, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on 1) Petition for Suspension of Powers and Removal of Jan Cherry Spears as Administrator; 2) Amended Petition for Letters of Administration; and 3) Order to Show Cause Why Administrator Should Not Be Suspended.

Ms. Allen requested appointment of Ms. Lawrence as successor administrator so the estate could be marshaled and closed, noting that the beneficiaries were prepared to proceed without requiring an accounting from Ms. Cherry Spears.

Ms. Hinshaw represented that her client had marshaled the estate assets, that they were held in accounts she could access, and that she was prepared to distribute them. She apologized for delays but assured the Court that her client was ready to complete administration.

The Court expressed concern over the lack of progress, noting that filings had not been made since 2005 despite partial fees already being taken.

Following discussion, the Court ordered the suspension of Jan Cherry Spears as administrator and appointed Karen Lawrence as special administrator with general powers pending further order. Ms. Cherry Spears was ordered to turn over all estate assets and records to Ms. Lawrence.

The Court discharged the Order to Show Cause regarding suspension, and indicated new Order to Show Cause shall be issued to suspended administrator Jan Cherry Spears to show why she should not be permanently removed as administrator and why Ms. Lawrence should not be appointed as successor general administrator. Hearing on the Order to Show Cause shall be set for November 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

The petition to suspend and remove Ms. Cherry Spears was taken off calendar as moot.

The amended petition for letters of administration was continued to November 18, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of Jan Cherry Spears As Administrator
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Continued To Sep-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Caroline Hinshaw for Administrator Jan Cherry (via Zoom).
Carla Allen for Proposed Successor Administrator Karen Lawrence, who was also present (via Zoom).
Eagle-Eye Cherry, beneficiary (via Zoom).
Christian Cherry, beneficiary (via Zoom).

The Court called the matter on two petitions: 1) petition for suspension of powers and removal of Jan Cherry Spears as administrator; and 2) amended petition for letters of administration.

The Court asked the parties to explain the delay in finalizing the settlement agreement discussed at prior hearings. Ms. Hinshaw acknowledged delays and indicated that settlement could still be reached. Ms. Allen stated that settlement discussions had occurred and that she believed resolution was possible if the administrator was willing to proceed. Ms. Lawrence expressed frustration that the case has been stagnant for so long, and that the administrator continued to delay without adequate explanation.

Ms. Allen argued that appointing Ms. Lawrence as administrator would allow the estate to be closed, as concerns regarding preservation of estate funds had been addressed.

Counsel discussed whether an accounting could be waived if the parties reached agreement. Ms. Lawrence expressed concern regarding the administrator's handling of accounts, including transfers between banks, and requested supporting documentation. Ms. Hinshaw assured the Court that information could be provided.

The Court noted its own frustration with the delays and emphasized the need for progress. The Court indicated that on its own motion, an Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause directed to Jan Cherry shall be issued regarding why she should not be suspended as administrator for failure to act. The Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause was set for hearing on September 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Both underlying petitions were also continued to the same date.

The Court advised that if a settlement agreement is finalized before that date, the matter will be taken off calendar. If not, the Court will remove Ms. Spears and appoint Ms. Lawrence as successor administrator.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Ms. Hinshaw reported that a draft settlement agreement had been received from opposing counsel, but she had not yet been able to speak with her client, who resides in Oklahoma and may be experiencing communication disruptions. Ms. Hinshaw requested additional time to confer with her client and revise the draft agreement.

Ms. Allen confirmed she had no objection to continuing the matter to allow time for settlement finalization.

The Court continued the matter to August 19, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

At the court's query, counsel reports as to the delay in closing this Estate with no specific reason provided, however, counsel reports of planning to meet to attempt to resolve the matters.
